assembly_instructions: Horizontal-axis wind turbine on a concrete foundation pad. A tall cylindrical tower rises from the pad and is held vertical by guy wires. A yaw bearing at the tower top carries a nacelle mount, a DC generator nacelle, a spherical rotor hub, and three slender blades at 120° around the hub. A tail vane projects rearward to weathervane the turbine into the wind; an anemometer-style sensor sits atop the nacelle. A power cable runs down the tower to a ground-level battery bank with a blocking diode.

info.japanese_name: 50 kW オープンソース風力タービン
info.english_name: Open Source Ecology 50 kW Wind Turbine
info.summary: Horizontal-axis, three-blade wind turbine on a guyed lattice/tube tower. Part of OSE's Global Village Construction Set as the renewable-electricity prime mover.
info.description: The OSE 50 kW wind turbine is a community-scale, three-blade horizontal-axis wind turbine (HAWT) published as part of the Global Village Construction Set. The rotor captures wind via three composite blades bolted to a central hub; the hub turns a direct-drive DC generator housed in the nacelle at the top of a tall tower. A tail vane behind the nacelle aligns the rotor into the wind by weathervaning around a yaw bearing, and a cup-style anemometer on the nacelle roof reports wind speed for cut-in/cut-out control. Direct-current output flows down a cable routed along the tower to a ground-level battery bank, through a blocking diode that prevents the battery from back-driving the generator in calm conditions. The tower is stabilized by guy wires anchored in the concrete foundation pad; OSE's documentation is released under CC BY-SA 4.0.
info.operator: OSE community builders and off-grid installations
info.status: Design / small-scale builds; 50 kW class is the roadmap target

facts.label: Tower
facts.value: Guyed tubular/lattice tower with concrete foundation pad

facts.label: Output
facts.value: DC → battery bank (with series blocking diode)

facts.label: Control
facts.value: Passive yaw via tail vane; furling / anemometer cut-out

sources.title: OSE — Global Village Construction Set overview

parts.name: Concrete Tower Base
parts.role: Reinforced concrete foundation pad that anchors the tower and the guy-wire turnbuckles.

parts.name: Tower
parts.role: Tapered tube/lattice tower lifting the rotor into cleaner airflow above terrain turbulence.

parts.name: Yaw Bearing
parts.role: Large-diameter thrust bearing at the tower top that lets the nacelle weathervane freely.

parts.name: Nacelle Mount
parts.role: Machined yaw-axis mount block that carries the nacelle and tail.

parts.name: DC Generator Nacelle
parts.role: Direct-drive DC generator. Converts rotor torque into electrical current.

parts.name: Rotor Hub
parts.role: Central rotor hub. Blades bolt on at 120° spacing; torque transfers into the generator shaft.

parts.name: Blade #1 (top)
parts.role: Composite aerodynamic blade pointing straight up.

parts.name: Blade #2 (lower-left)
parts.role: Composite aerodynamic blade at 120° around the rotor disk.

parts.name: Blade #3 (lower-right)
parts.role: Composite aerodynamic blade at 240° around the rotor disk.

parts.name: Tail Vane
parts.role: Rear vertical fin that yaws the rotor into the wind.

parts.name: Anemometer / Wind Sensor
parts.role: Cup anemometer + wind-vane sensor. Feeds cut-in/cut-out logic and data logging.

parts.name: DC Power Cable
parts.role: Insulated DC feeder routed along the tower from the generator to the battery bank.

parts.name: Battery Bank
parts.role: Ground-level battery bank that stores harvested energy.

parts.name: Blocking Diode
parts.role: Series blocking diode. Prevents the battery from back-driving the generator when wind drops.

parts.name: Guy Wire (representative)
parts.role: Steel guy wire from an upper tower collar to a ground anchor. Three such guys at 120° around the tower stabilize it against lateral wind loads; one is modeled here as representative.
